hero.primaryPattern: Type 2 diabetes involves insulin resistance, high blood sugar, beta-cell strain, metabolic risk, and long-term heart, kidney, nerve, eye, and circulation complications.
hero.safetyPreview: Confusion, severe weakness, vomiting, fruity breath, deep breathing, severe dehydration, very high/low sugar symptoms, chest pain, or infection signs need urgent care.

biologyPathway.title: Insulin Resistance
biologyPathway.detail: Insulin helps glucose enter cells. In type 2 diabetes, cells resist the signal, so glucose can remain high in the bloodstream.

biologyPathway.title: Beta-Cell Strain
biologyPathway.detail: The pancreas may produce extra insulin at first, then struggle over time as beta-cell function declines.

biologyPathway.title: Vascular and Nerve Risk
biologyPathway.detail: Long-term high blood sugar can injure blood vessels, nerves, eyes, kidneys, heart, feet, and wound healing.

biologyPathway.title: Monitoring
biologyPathway.detail: A1c, glucose, blood pressure, cholesterol, kidney function, eye exams, foot checks, meds, food, and activity all matter. Annoying paperwork, lifesaving point.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Metabolic Disease
body: Type 2 diabetes is medically a chronic metabolic disease involving insulin resistance and high blood glucose.
It is not just sugar, not just weight, and not proof that your character went bankrupt.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Processing Glucose
body: Insulin helps move glucose from blood into cells.
When cells resist insulin, glucose builds up in the bloodstream and the pancreas may have to work harder.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Complication Prevention
body: Long-term management reduces risk to the heart, kidneys, nerves, eyes, feet, circulation, and wound healing.
A1c, blood pressure, cholesterol, kidney function, eye exams, foot care, meds, nutrition, and activity are not glamorous, just important.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Emergency Signs
body: Confusion, severe weakness, vomiting, fruity breath, deep breathing, severe dehydration, very high or low blood sugar symptoms, chest pain, or serious infection signs need urgent care.
Do not spiritualize diabetic emergencies. That is how humans get turned into cautionary slides.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Sugar Processing Plant With Jammed Doors
paragraphs: Your metabolism is a sugar processing plant where insulin keeps knocking on cell doors.
The workers used to answer. Now the doors stick, the conveyor belt jams, and glucose is piling up like boxes in a warehouse run by raccoons.
The pancreas is the overworked manager printing more insulin memos while the workers stop reading emails.
Cortisol and stress keep yelling WORK HARDER, which is not a treatment plan, though middle management keeps trying it.
The factory needs real management: labs, meds if prescribed, food rhythm, movement, sleep, blood pressure, cholesterol, kidney checks, eyes, and feet.
The factory can improve. It just cannot be run on denial and cinnamon folklore.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Over-Processing Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a metabolic problem. You may also have an over-processing pattern.
Medical first: Type 2 diabetes is serious. Track A1c/glucose. Take meds as directed. Watch heart, kidney, nerve, eye, and foot risk.
Symbolically, ask what you keep trying to metabolize that is not actually nourishing.
What obligations are you swallowing? What demands are being sold to you as sweetness? What yes keeps turning into metabolic sludge?
Do not stop meds. Do not ignore symptoms. Do not confuse self-reflection with glucose control.
Receive real nourishment. Release non-nourishing loads. Manage the actual disease.
Process the real load or keep making your pancreas work unpaid overtime. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen-Pancreas Processing
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine imagery, Spleen relates to transformation and nourishment.
Symbolically, diabetes can mirror a processing system overwhelmed by what it has to handle.

influenceLayers.title: Sweetness and Nourishment
paragraphs: Sweetness can symbolize love, ease, and nourishment.
The question is whether what is being taken in is actually nourishing.

influenceLayers.title: Consumed Self
paragraphs: The Consumed Self keeps accepting what exceeds capacity.
The healthier pattern is enough, not endless intake.

influenceLayers.title: Syrupy Load
paragraphs: The glucose backlog becomes an image for unprocessed load.
Medically, it is blood sugar and needs actual care.

influenceLayers.title: Process and Release
paragraphs: The reset is medical management plus reducing non-nourishing burdens.
The pancreas is not your emotional unpaid intern.

reset.title: Metabolic Grounding & Processing Reset
steps: Sit upright with one hand on the lower belly and one on the chest.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I can receive what nourishes me and release what I cannot process.
Then choose one practical action: check glucose if directed, take meds, walk, plan a balanced meal, or schedule labs.

naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Type 2 diabetes requires medical monitoring, medication guidance, and individualized care.

items.label: Monitor
items.detail: Track glucose/A1c as directed, plus blood pressure, cholesterol, kidney function, eyes, and feet.

items.label: Medication adherence
items.detail: Take prescribed medications as directed and do not stop without medical guidance.

items.label: Food rhythm
items.detail: Protein, fiber, whole foods, hydration, and carbohydrate awareness can support glucose stability.

items.label: Movement
items.detail: Regular movement can improve insulin sensitivity when safe and medically appropriate.

avoid: Do not ignore confusion, vomiting, fruity breath, deep breathing, severe weakness, dehydration, severe high/low sugar symptoms, chest pain, or infection signs.
Do not stop meds or replace care with supplements.

brutalActions: Track A1c/glucose like numbers are allowed to matter.
Take prescribed meds.
Move after meals when safe.
Stop saying yes to loads your body keeps reporting as too much.

clinical.mechanism: Type 2 diabetes is a chronic metabolic condition involving high blood glucose, insulin resistance, and relative insulin deficiency over time. It increases risk for heart disease, stroke, kidney disease, neuropathy, vision problems, wounds, infections, and other complications.
protocol: Monitor A1c/glucose, blood pressure, cholesterol, kidney function, eyes, feet, medications, nutrition, activity, and symptoms.
Do not stop or adjust diabetes medication without medical guidance.
Seek urgent care for confusion, vomiting, fruity breath, deep breathing, severe dehydration, severe high/low sugar symptoms, chest pain, or serious infection.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for diabetes care.

witty.metaphorTitle: The Sugar Processing Plant With Jammed Doors

witty.wittyAdvice: Fix the factory with actual care, not cinnamon mythology.

biologyPath.title: Stress Load
biologyPath.description: Your system detects chronic emotional conflict or survival anxiety, starting the alarm.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Under perpetual demands—real or manufactured by overthinking—the amygdala sounds a continuous warning, preventing the nervous system from entering the restorative parasympathetic state.

biologyPath.title: Cortisol Rise
biologyPath.description: The HPA axis floods your bloodstream with stress hormones, preparing you to run.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Your stress hormone. It tells the liver to release glucose because your body thinks danger is near. Cortisol also makes cells less receptive to insulin, reserving glucose in the blood for immediate physical defense.

biologyPath.title: Liver Glucose Output
biologyPath.description: The liver dumps stored sugar to feed muscles for a fight that never actually happens.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: Normally, the liver stores glucose as glycogen. Under stress, it undergoes gluconeogenesis, turning proteins and fats into extra blood sugar, believing you need immediate muscular energy to fight off a saber-toothed predator (which is actually just an email).

biologyPath.title: Insulin Resistance
biologyPath.description: Overwhelmed cells shut their doors to protect themselves from the glucose flood.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: As blood sugar remains chronically high, insulin is constantly pumped out to clear it. Overworked cells downregulate their receptors, effectively jamming the 'locks' so insulin can no longer deliver sugar inside. Sugar backs up in the bloodstream.

biologyPath.title: Beta-Cell Overload
biologyPath.description: The pancreas burns out trying to produce double the insulin to force the locked doors.
biologyPath.expandedDetails: The beta-cells in your pancreas work overtime, screaming to produce more insulin. Over years, this hyper-secretion may contribute to mitochondrial damage, beta-cell exhaustion, and eventually, a decline in total insulin production capability.

biologyPathway.title: Hunger Signal
biologyPathway.short: Appetite can rise for many reasons.
biologyPathway.detail: Hunger is shaped by glucose, hormones, sleep, medications, stress, mood, digestion, and brain signaling.

biologyPathway.title: Medical Causes
biologyPathway.detail: Diabetes, hypoglycemia, hyperthyroidism, pregnancy, medications, neurologic conditions, and rare genetic syndromes can cause excessive appetite.

biologyPathway.title: Satiety Disruption
biologyPathway.detail: Ghrelin, leptin, insulin, cortisol, sleep disruption, and emotional stress can affect appetite and cravings.

biologyPathway.title: Emotional Hunger
biologyPathway.detail: Food may soothe, distract, numb, or temporarily fill an emotional need, but it cannot replace connection, rest, safety, or treatment.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Hyperphagia Is a Symptom
body: Excessive appetite is a symptom, not a moral failure.
Possible causes include diabetes, hypoglycemia, hyperthyroidism, medications, sleep disruption, pregnancy/hormones, neurologic conditions, mood disorders, eating disorders, and stress.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Hunger Hormones and Blood Sugar
body: Appetite is influenced by glucose, insulin, ghrelin, leptin, cortisol, sleep, and brain reward pathways.
The body can demand food when energy use, blood sugar, stress, medication effects, or satiety signaling gets disrupted.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Emotional Eating Without Shame
body: Food can become a substitute for safety, connection, rest, comfort, or relief.
That does not make you weak. It means the warehouse has been using snacks as emergency infrastructure, because apparently the city planner quit.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Red Flag Check
body: Excessive thirst, frequent urination, unexplained weight loss, vision changes, tremors, rapid heartbeat, fainting, purging, severe distress, or sudden major appetite change needs medical evaluation.
Do not turn diabetes symptoms into a journal prompt.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Warehouse With a Broken Full Sensor
paragraphs: Your appetite system is a warehouse with a full sensor that has been lying to management.
The shelves may be full, but the sensor keeps yelling WE NEED MORE INVENTORY.
Cortisol and Adrenaline are panicked supervisors ordering emergency shipments because a famine might arrive. The famine is not currently on the schedule.
Boxes clog the aisles. The forklift driver is crying. Someone labeled one crate CONNECTION and then filled it with chips.
Sometimes this is medical. Sometimes it is emotional. Sometimes it is medication, blood sugar, thyroid, sleep, or stress being a committee of goblins.
The job is not shame. The job is inventory.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Hollow Hunger Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have an excessive appetite problem. You may also have a hunger-you-have-not-named pattern.
Medical first: blood sugar, thyroid, meds, sleep, mood, eating disorder patterns, pregnancy/hormones, and neurologic causes matter.
Symbolically, ask what you are actually hungry for.
Connection? Rest? Safety? Permission to stop? Comfort? Control? Relief?
Food can help hunger. It cannot become your entire emotional supply chain without consequences.
Name the hunger, check the body, and build a real source of nourishment.
Name the hunger or keep sending emergency shipments to a warehouse that is already full. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen-Stomach Signal

Symbolically, excessive appetite can mirror a system asking for nourishment it cannot quite receive.

influenceLayers.title: False Hunger Heat
paragraphs: Heat can symbolize restless reaching and dissatisfaction.
The body may feel like it keeps asking even after food arrives.

influenceLayers.title: Insatiable Self
paragraphs: The Insatiable Self keeps reaching because the true need has not been named.
The answer is not punishment. It is discernment.

influenceLayers.title: Warehouse Inventory
paragraphs: This layer asks whether the hunger is physical, emotional, medical, or situational.
Different hunger needs different food.

influenceLayers.title: Name the Hunger
paragraphs: The reset is pausing long enough to identify the need.
Then feed the correct system instead of dumping boxes into the warehouse.

reset.title: Appetite Grounding & Hunger Naming
steps: Sit upright with one hand on the stomach and one hand on the chest.

Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I can name what I need. Food is one form of nourishment, not the only one.
Then ask: is this body hunger, emotional hunger, habit hunger, or a medical signal?

biologyPathway.title: Lipid Transport
biologyPathway.detail: LDL, triglyceride-rich particles, HDL, non-HDL cholesterol, ApoB, and Lp(a) can help define cardiovascular risk.

biologyPathway.title: Plaque Formation
biologyPathway.short: Atherogenic particles can enter artery walls.
biologyPathway.detail: Over time, certain cholesterol-containing particles can contribute to plaque buildup and narrowed or unstable arteries.

biologyPathway.title: Risk Context
biologyPathway.detail: Age, blood pressure, diabetes, smoking, family history, kidney disease, inflammation, prior events, and LDL level affect treatment decisions.

biologyPathway.title: Management
biologyPathway.short: Lifestyle and meds may both matter.
biologyPathway.detail: Nutrition, activity, weight, smoking cessation, sleep, treating diabetes/thyroid issues, and lipid-lowering medication can reduce risk when appropriate.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Lipids in Blood
body: High cholesterol/hyperlipidemia means blood lipids or atherogenic particles are elevated enough to raise concern.
LDL cholesterol and triglycerides matter, but overall cardiovascular risk context matters too.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Artery Risk
body: Atherogenic particles can contribute to plaque buildup in artery walls.
That raises risk for heart attack, stroke, and peripheral artery disease over time.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Causes and Contributors
body: Genetics, diet, diabetes, thyroid disease, kidney/liver disease, medications, alcohol, smoking, menopause, and family history can affect lipid levels.
Stress may worsen behaviors and physiology in some people, but it is not the sole explanation. Cholesterol is annoyingly material.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Treatment Planning
body: Management depends on lipid panel, ASCVD risk, family history, diabetes, blood pressure, smoking, prior events, and clinician judgment.
Medication is not moral failure. Sometimes it is pipe maintenance with better chemistry.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Grease Trap in the Basement
paragraphs: Your cardiovascular system is an old building with pipes that were never meant to host a grease parade.
The liver-plumber is overworked. LDL particles are carrying cargo. Triglycerides are making the hallway slippery.
The landlord keeps saying WE'RE FINE while the building inspector tapes another note to the boiler room door.
Some sludge comes from genetics. Some from diet. Some from diabetes, thyroid issues, meds, alcohol, smoking, or age. The pipes are a group project.
The cleanout plan is labs, risk score, food pattern, movement, smoking status, glucose, blood pressure, and meds if needed.
The grease trap does not empty itself because you forgave your uncle in a journal. Touching, but insufficient.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Buildup Reality Check
paragraphs: You do have a high cholesterol problem. You may also have an accumulation pattern.
Medical first: high LDL/triglycerides can raise heart attack and stroke risk. Get labs. Know your risk. Treat it.
Symbolically, ask what has been building up.
What resentment, grief, stress, habit, or avoidance has become sludge in your life?
Release matters emotionally. But arteries also need nutrition, movement, not smoking, diabetes control, blood pressure control, and sometimes medication.
Do not confuse letting go with lipid management. Do both.
Clean the grease trap or keep asking narrow pipes to host a sludge parade. Your choice.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — ED Is a Symptom
body: Erectile dysfunction is persistent difficulty getting or maintaining an erection sufficient for sex.
It can be vascular, neurologic, endocrine, medication-related, psychological, relationship-related, or mixed.

body: An erection is largely a vascular event requiring blood-vessel relaxation and adequate blood flow.
ED can sometimes be an early sign of diabetes, high blood pressure, atherosclerosis, or cardiovascular disease.

body: Anxiety and stress can push the body toward sympathetic activation, which is bad timing for erection physiology.
Trying harder often makes the system more threatened, because apparently the penis did not request a motivational speech.

witty.title: The Helium Tank and the Panicked Party Balloon
paragraphs: Your erection system is a party balloon with a helium tank controlled by blood flow, nerves, hormones, mood, sleep, meds, and stress.
Cortisol and Adrenaline are panicked supervisors guarding the tank while yelling THIS BETTER GO WELL.
That is, naturally, the exact attitude that makes the party balloon reconsider its career.
Sometimes the tank is low from vascular disease, diabetes, testosterone issues, medication effects, alcohol, sleep apnea, or depression.
Sometimes the tank is fine but the room feels like a performance review with naked consequences.
The goal is not yelling at the balloon. It is checking the tank, lowering the pressure, and returning to presence.

What makes you feel watched instead of wanted? Where are you trying to prove instead of feel?
Get the medical workup. Talk honestly. Take pressure off the performance.
Take pressure off the stage or keep asking a vascular system to perform under threat lighting. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Essence
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine, Kidney imagery is linked with sexual vitality, fear, and deep reserve.
Symbolically, ED can mirror depletion or fear around adequacy.

influenceLayers.title: Heart-Pelvis Connection

Performance pressure splits attention away from sensation.

influenceLayers.title: Hidden Self

Healing is not proving. It is safe showing up.

influenceLayers.title: Threat Lighting
paragraphs: When the body thinks it is being evaluated, blood flow may not cooperate.
The body is many things. A stage prop is not one of them.

influenceLayers.title: Presence Over Performance
paragraphs: The reset is medical care, pressure reduction, communication, and nervous-system downshift.
Presence is not pass/fail.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care, Lifestyle, and Supplement Caution
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. ED can be an early cardiovascular, diabetes, medication, hormonal, neurologic, sleep, mental-health, or relationship signal. Supplements can interact with medications and are not automatically safe.

items.label: Medical review first
items.detail: Ask about blood pressure, diabetes/A1c, cholesterol, cardiovascular risk, testosterone if indicated, thyroid/prolactin if indicated, sleep apnea, medications, alcohol/substances, and mood.

items.label: Exercise
items.detail: Regular moderate activity supports blood flow, cardiovascular health, glucose control, mood, and erectile function.

items.label: Mediterranean-style diet
items.detail: Fruits, vegetables, legumes, whole grains, fish, olive oil, nuts, and less ultra-processed food support vascular health.

items.label: Sleep
items.detail: Poor sleep can worsen testosterone, stress hormones, mood, and sexual function.

items.label: Smoking and alcohol
items.detail: Smoking damages blood vessels. Alcohol can impair erectile response and worsen sleep.

items.label: Panax ginseng
items.detail: Some studies suggest possible benefit, but dosing, product quality, side effects, and interactions matter.
items.caution: May cause insomnia, headaches, dizziness, or interact with medications. Ask a clinician first.

items.label: Saffron
items.detail: Some small studies suggest possible sexual-function benefit, but evidence is limited and product quality varies.
items.caution: Avoid high-dose experimentation without medical guidance.

items.label: L-arginine
items.detail: May support nitric-oxide pathways in some people.
items.caution: Can cause GI side effects and may interact with blood-pressure meds, nitrates, PDE5 inhibitors, or heart conditions. Do not stack casually with Viagra/Cialis.

items.label: Avoid yohimbe
items.detail: Yohimbe can raise blood pressure, trigger fast or irregular heartbeat, anxiety, seizures, or serious cardiovascular problems.
items.caution: Do not use without medical supervision.

items.label: Avoid mystery enhancement pills
items.detail: Many so-called natural sexual-performance products have hidden drug-like ingredients or unsafe dosing.
items.caution: Especially dangerous with heart disease, nitrates, blood-pressure medication, diabetes, or multiple meds.

avoid: Do not ignore ED as purely psychological, especially with diabetes, high blood pressure, cholesterol, smoking, chest symptoms, or cardiovascular risk.
Do not use nitrates with PDE5 inhibitors like sildenafil/tadalafil.
Do not buy gas-station or online mystery ED pills.
Do not treat supplements as safer than prescriptions just because the bottle has a leaf on it. Leaves have committed crimes.

brutalActions: Check cardiovascular and diabetes risk.
Review meds, alcohol, sleep, and testosterone if indicated.
Use lifestyle support before supplement roulette.
Avoid yohimbe and mystery boner pills like they owe you money.

clinical.mechanism: Erectile dysfunction is difficulty getting or maintaining an erection sufficient for sex. It can involve cardiovascular disease, diabetes, high blood pressure, vascular disease, nerve injury, low testosterone, thyroid or prolactin problems, depression, anxiety, relationship stress, medications, alcohol/substance use, sleep apnea, pelvic surgery, or Peyronie's disease.
protocol: Seek medical review for new, persistent, sudden, or distressing ED.
Check cardiovascular/metabolic risk, diabetes, blood pressure, medications, hormones if indicated, sleep, mood, and relationship factors.
Seek urgent care for chest pain, shortness of breath, severe testicular pain, neurologic symptoms, or concerning urinary changes.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for medical evaluation.

biologyPathway.title: Hormone Transition
biologyPathway.detail: Perimenopause can involve years of hormone fluctuation before menopause is confirmed after 12 months without a period.

biologyPathway.title: Vasomotor Symptoms
biologyPathway.short: Hot flashes and night sweats happen.
biologyPathway.detail: Hormone changes can affect the brain's temperature regulation, producing sudden heat, sweating, chills, and sleep disruption.

biologyPathway.title: Mood, Sleep, and Body Changes
biologyPathway.detail: Sleep, mood, cognition, libido, vaginal and urinary tissues, joints, skin, weight distribution, and migraines may shift.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Menopause and Perimenopause
body: Menopause is diagnosed after 12 consecutive months without a menstrual period.
Perimenopause can last years and may bring irregular cycles, hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, mood shifts, and body changes.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Thermostat Narrowing
body: Estrogen shifts can affect the hypothalamus and narrow the body's temperature comfort zone.
That can create hot flashes, sweating, chills, and night waking, because apparently the HVAC system wanted drama.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Options
body: Management can include lifestyle changes, hormone therapy for some people, non-hormonal medications, vaginal therapies, sleep support, mood care, and bone/heart screening.
Treatment choice depends on symptoms, risk factors, uterus status, bleeding pattern, medical history, and clinician guidance.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Postmenopausal bleeding, heavy or prolonged bleeding, severe depression/anxiety, self-harm thoughts, chest pain, neurologic symptoms, or severe impairment needs medical care.
Natural transition does not mean suffer silently until the thermostat starts smoking.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Thermostat War
paragraphs: Your body is a building with a thermostat that has lost faith in reality.
The heat turns on during summer. The AC turns on during winter. The occupants are sweaty, cold, annoyed, and deeply unimpressed.
Estrogen and progesterone are retiring managers who left no forwarding address.
The hypothalamus keeps yelling IS IT HOT? IS IT COLD? LET'S DO BOTH.
The maintenance team needs calibration: sleep support, symptom tracking, medical options, and fewer people telling you this is just aging.
The building is not doomed. It needs a new operating manual.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Transition Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have menopause symptoms. You may also have an identity-transition pattern.
Medical first: hot flashes, bleeding changes, sleep disruption, mood symptoms, vaginal dryness, urinary symptoms, bone risk, and heart risk deserve real care.
Symbolically, ask what role is ending and what new self is trying to come online.
Menopause is not proof you are disappearing. It is proof the old operating system is being replaced while you are still using the device.
Grieve what is ending. Treat what needs treating. Build the next chapter with actual support.
Calibrate the thermostat or keep letting the building run heat and AC at the same time. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney Jing Transition

influenceLayers.title: Yin-Yang Recalibration

The work is cooling, anchoring, and adjusting the new rhythm.

influenceLayers.title: Wise Woman Archetype

She is authority after approval stops being the main currency.

influenceLayers.title: Role Release

The building needs recalibration, not demolition.

Body composition, waist circumference, metabolic labs, function, symptoms, and individual history matter.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Overstuffed House
paragraphs: Your body is a house that has accumulated boxes for years.
Some boxes are genetics. Some are meds. Some are stress. Some are sleep debt. Some are pain. Some are food patterns. Some are emotional junk mail from 2009.
The closets are full and the attic is rude.
The answer is not burning the house down. The answer is sorting boxes with medical help, realistic routines, and less shame from people who think metabolism is a motivational quote.
One box at a time leaves the house.
The house deserves maintenance, not contempt.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest

Medical first: weight is affected by biology, environment, meds, sleep, stress, hormones, access, pain, mental health, and energy balance.
Symbolically, ask what you are carrying.
What obligation, grief, fear, habit, injury, medication, or survival pattern added boxes to the house?
Shame is useless. Avoidance is expensive. A plan beats self-hatred every time.
Check the medical causes. Build sustainable routines. Remove one box.
Clean one room of the house or keep renting storage units for things you never wanted. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Dampness

influenceLayers.title: Liver Stagnation

influenceLayers.title: The Accumulator

The Releaser learns what can leave safely.

influenceLayers.title: Protective Buffer

That meaning may be useful, but it is not the only cause.

influenceLayers.title: One Box Leaves

No crash diets. No shame rituals. Humanity has suffered enough.

Remove one box from the house today.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Infertility Is Medical, Not Moral
body: Infertility is difficulty conceiving after regular unprotected sex over a defined period, often 12 months or sooner with age/history factors.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Causes to Check

Testing both partners is basic sanity, not blame distribution.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Grief and Pressure
body: Infertility can bring grief, shame, anger, jealousy, exhaustion, and relationship strain.
Emotional support is not optional decoration. This process can chew people up if they try to white-knuckle it.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Options
body: Options can include ovulation tracking, medications, surgery, IUI, IVF, donor gametes, gestational carrier, adoption, or choosing a child-free path.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty

paragraphs: Your reproductive system is a garden, and the garden is not blooming on command.
Maybe the timing is off. Maybe the soil needs testing. Maybe the pollen is weak. Maybe the irrigation system is blocked. Maybe the weather is a clown show.
The worst gardener in the world says JUST RELAX. That gardener should be escorted off the property.
The garden needs evaluation: ovulation, sperm, tubes, uterus, hormones, history, and sometimes a specialist with expensive acronyms.
Longing is real. Grief is real. So is medical testing.
Do not let the empty garden become proof you are empty.

hero.primaryPattern: Thrush/candidiasis may involve Candida overgrowth, antibiotics, diabetes, immune suppression, steroid inhalers, dentures, dry mouth, hormones, pregnancy, or local irritation.
hero.safetyPreview: Fever, severe pain, trouble swallowing, immune suppression, diabetes symptoms, recurrent thrush, pregnancy, or worsening after treatment needs medical care.

biologyPathway.title: Candida Balance
biologyPathway.short: Candida can live normally in small amounts.
biologyPathway.detail: Candida may exist in the mouth, gut, skin, or vagina without causing disease until conditions allow overgrowth.

biologyPathway.title: Overgrowth Triggers
biologyPathway.short: Antibiotics, diabetes, immune suppression, and steroids matter.
biologyPathway.detail: Broad-spectrum antibiotics, high blood sugar, immune suppression, steroid inhalers, dry mouth, dentures, pregnancy, hormones, and irritation can increase risk.

biologyPathway.title: Symptoms by Location
biologyPathway.detail: Oral thrush can cause white patches, soreness, cracking, or swallowing pain. Vaginal yeast can cause itching, burning, redness, soreness, and thick white discharge.

biologyPathway.title: Recurrent Screen
biologyPathway.detail: Recurrent or severe candidiasis can point to diabetes, immune suppression, medication effects, or resistant/recurrent infection.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Candida Overgrowth
body: Thrush/candidiasis is Candida yeast overgrowth.
It can affect the mouth, vagina, skin folds, nipples, or other body areas.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Why It Happens
body: Risk factors can include antibiotics, diabetes/high blood sugar, immune suppression, steroid inhalers, dentures, dry mouth, pregnancy/hormonal changes, irritation, and some medications.
Candida is opportunistic, because apparently even yeast understands timing.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Treatment Depends on Location
body: Treatment may involve antifungal medication and correcting contributing factors.
Rinse after steroid inhalers, manage blood sugar, address denture fit/hygiene, and finish prescribed treatment when directed.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek care for fever, severe pain, trouble swallowing, immune suppression, diabetes symptoms, recurrent thrush, pregnancy, spreading rash, or worsening after treatment.
Repeated yeast problems deserve investigation, not another round of shrugging.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Overgrown Candida Garden
paragraphs: Your body is a garden where Candida was supposed to be a minor background plant.
Then antibiotics, blood sugar, dry mouth, hormones, steroids, dentures, immune stress, or irritation opened the gate.
Now the weeds have a newsletter and a parking permit.
The flowers are still there. The terrain just needs help.
That help may be antifungal treatment, blood sugar review, inhaler rinsing, denture care, trigger tracking, or medical evaluation.
Do not hand the garden to miracle detox people. They will sell you compost and fear.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Overgrowth Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a thrush problem. You may also have an overgrowth-and-depletion pattern.
Medical first: Candida overgrowth can point to antibiotics, diabetes, immune suppression, steroid inhalers, dry mouth, dentures, hormones, pregnancy, or irritation.
Symbolically, ask what small thing has been allowed to take over.
What obligation, habit, sweetness, resentment, or exhaustion has spread past its proper place?
Treat the yeast. Check the terrain. Restore balance without declaring war on your body.
Restore the terrain or keep letting the yeast open a garden center. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Spleen Damp-Heat
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine language, damp-heat can symbolize sticky overgrowth and irritation.
Medically, Candida still requires appropriate diagnosis and treatment.

influenceLayers.title: Balanced Self
paragraphs: The Balanced Self knows when enough is enough.
Overgrowth asks where proportion has been lost.

influenceLayers.title: Garden Terrain
paragraphs: The garden image focuses on terrain, not blame.
You are not dirty. The ecosystem needs adjustment.

influenceLayers.title: Sweetness and Yeast
paragraphs: High blood sugar can support yeast growth.
Symbolically, sweetness without balance can feed what should stay small.

influenceLayers.title: Restore Balance
paragraphs: The reset is antifungal care, risk-factor review, and reducing what feeds recurrence.
Weeds get pulled. Soil gets repaired.

reset.title: Candida Terrain & Balance Reset
steps: Sit upright with one hand on the lower belly and one on the upper chest.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I restore balance. I release what has grown beyond what is needed.
Then choose one practical action: take prescribed antifungal, rinse after inhaler use, review blood sugar, clean dentures, or schedule care for recurrence.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Thrush/candidiasis may need antifungal treatment and evaluation for underlying causes.

items.label: Treat appropriately
items.detail: Use antifungal treatment as directed for the location and severity.

items.label: Review risk factors
items.detail: Antibiotics, diabetes, steroid inhalers, dentures, dry mouth, pregnancy, hormones, and immune status may matter.

items.label: Rinse after inhalers
items.detail: Rinsing the mouth after inhaled steroids can reduce oral thrush risk.

items.label: Check recurrence
items.detail: Recurrent thrush deserves evaluation for diabetes, immune suppression, medication effects, or resistant infection.

avoid: Do not ignore trouble swallowing, fever, severe pain, immune suppression, diabetes symptoms, pregnancy, spreading rash, or recurrent symptoms.
Do not treat every genital symptom as yeast without testing.

brutalActions: Treat the yeast correctly.
Check blood sugar if symptoms recur.
Rinse after steroid inhalers.
Pull one overgrown obligation out by the roots.

protocol: Treat with appropriate antifungal medication when diagnosed.
Review diabetes, immune status, antibiotics, steroid inhalers, dentures, dry mouth, hormones, pregnancy, and recurrence.
Seek care for severe pain, trouble swallowing, fever, immune suppression, recurrent thrush, or worsening after treatment.

hero.primaryPattern: Thyroid problems may involve low or high thyroid hormone, autoimmune disease, nodules, thyroiditis, medication effects, iodine, pregnancy/postpartum changes, pituitary issues, or structural thyroid disease.
hero.safetyPreview: Chest pain, rapid heartbeat, shortness of breath, fever, confusion, severe weakness, eye symptoms, neck swelling, pregnancy, or medication changes need care.

biologyPathway.title: Thyroid Hormones
biologyPathway.short: T4 and T3 regulate metabolic pace.
biologyPathway.detail: The thyroid makes hormones that influence energy use, heart rate, temperature, digestion, mood, skin, hair, and many other systems.

biologyPathway.title: Hypothyroid Pattern
biologyPathway.detail: Hypothyroidism can cause fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hair loss, depression, weight gain, heavy periods, and slow heart rate.

biologyPathway.title: Hyperthyroid Pattern
biologyPathway.detail: Hyperthyroidism can cause weight loss, heat intolerance, anxiety, tremor, palpitations, diarrhea, insomnia, sweating, and eye symptoms in Graves' disease.

biologyPathway.title: Labs and Treatment
biologyPathway.detail: TSH and free T4 are common starting labs; treatment depends on the cause. Please do not let vibes manage endocrine chemistry.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Thyroid Regulates Pace
body: The thyroid produces hormones that influence metabolism, temperature, heart rate, digestion, mood, skin, hair, and energy.
Problems can be underactive, overactive, inflammatory, autoimmune, nodular, medication-related, postpartum, or pituitary-related.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Too Slow vs Too Fast
body: Hypothyroidism can feel like fatigue, coldness, constipation, depression, weight gain, dry skin, and brain fog.
Hyperthyroidism can feel like anxiety, heat, weight loss, palpitations, tremor, insomnia, sweating, and frequent bowel movements.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Lab-Guided Care
body: Thyroid care is guided by labs, symptoms, cause, pregnancy status, medications, and exam findings.
Do not stop, start, or change thyroid medication without medical guidance. The gland is dramatic enough already.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Emergency Check
body: Chest pain, severe rapid heartbeat, shortness of breath, fever, confusion, agitation, fainting, severe weakness, eye pain/vision changes, or major neck swelling needs urgent care.
Thyroid storm and severe hypothyroid states are not self-care projects.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Broken Thermostat With a Clipboard
paragraphs: Your thyroid is the thermostat for the metabolic building.
Sometimes it runs the heat in July. Sometimes it turns the entire building into a walk-in freezer and calls it efficiency.
T3 and T4 are the maintenance crew. TSH is the increasingly frustrated email from management.
When the system runs too slow, everyone moves like wet cement. When it runs too fast, the elevators start screaming.
The fix is labs, medication management, cause review, and not letting internet strangers adjust your dose with the confidence of medieval barbers.
The building can be recalibrated. First, stop arguing with the thermostat and test the wires.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Pace Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a thyroid problem. You may also have a pace-and-rhythm pattern.
Medical first: thyroid problems require labs and real treatment. Autoimmune disease, nodules, meds, iodine, pregnancy, and pituitary issues can all matter.
Symbolically, ask who has been setting your pace.
Are you rushing to prove yourself? Frozen from burnout? Swinging between sprint and collapse?
Find your rhythm, yes. But also check TSH/free T4 and take medication correctly if prescribed.
Check the thermostat with labs or keep letting the building argue with its own HVAC. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Kidney-Stomach Rhythm
paragraphs: In symbolic Chinese medicine language, thyroid imbalance can be mapped to deep reserve and transformation.
Medically, thyroid hormones and labs remain the actual steering wheel.

influenceLayers.title: Throat Expression
paragraphs: The thyroid sits at the throat, making it symbolically tied to voice and pace.
The question becomes: whose timing are you living by?

influenceLayers.title: Rushed Self
paragraphs: The Rushed Self fears slowing down.
The Frozen Self fears moving again.

influenceLayers.title: Thermostat War
paragraphs: This layer imagines hyper and hypo as speed conflicts.
Testing determines which one is actually happening.

influenceLayers.title: Lab-Guided Calibration
paragraphs: The reset is rhythm, medication safety, and medical monitoring.
Your pace matters. So do the numbers.

reset.title: Thyroid Pace & Thermostat Calibration
steps: Sit upright with one hand near the throat and one on the lower belly.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I find my own pace. I listen to my body and check what needs checking.
Then choose one practical action: take meds correctly, schedule labs, track symptoms, or call for urgent care if red flags are present.

naturalSupport.title: Supportive Care
naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Thyroid disorders require lab-based diagnosis and medical treatment when present.

items.label: Lab monitoring
items.detail: TSH and free T4 are commonly used; free T3, antibodies, ultrasound, or other tests may be ordered depending on context.

items.label: Medication consistency
items.detail: Take thyroid medication exactly as directed and watch interactions with food, calcium, iron, antacids, and other meds.

items.label: Symptom tracking
items.detail: Track heart rate, weight, temperature tolerance, mood, sleep, bowel changes, skin/hair, periods, and neck/eye symptoms.

items.label: Cause review
items.detail: Ask about Hashimoto's, Graves', thyroiditis, nodules, pregnancy/postpartum, iodine, medications, and pituitary issues when relevant.

avoid: Do not change thyroid medication without medical guidance.
Do not ignore chest pain, severe rapid heartbeat, fever/confusion, shortness of breath, fainting, severe weakness, eye symptoms, or neck swelling.

brutalActions: Get labs instead of guessing.
Take thyroid meds correctly if prescribed.
Track symptoms like a grown-up with a malfunctioning thermostat.
Stop letting everyone else set your pace.

clinical.mechanism: Thyroid problems include hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, thyroiditis, nodules, goiter, autoimmune Hashimoto's or Graves' disease, medication effects, iodine issues, pregnancy/postpartum changes, pituitary issues, or thyroid cancer. Symptoms depend on whether hormone levels are low, high, fluctuating, or structurally abnormal.
protocol: Track symptoms and use lab-guided evaluation with a clinician.
Do not adjust thyroid medication without medical guidance.
Seek urgent care for chest pain, severe rapid heartbeat, shortness of breath, fever/confusion, fainting, severe weakness, eye symptoms, or neck swelling.
citations: Educational reflection only; not a substitute for thyroid/endocrine care.

hero.primaryPattern: Vaginitis may involve BV, yeast, trichomoniasis, STIs, irritants, low estrogen, antibiotics, diabetes, hormones, pregnancy, immune changes, or microbiome disruption.
hero.safetyPreview: Fever, pelvic pain, pregnancy, foul odor, green/yellow discharge, bleeding, sores, STI concern, severe pain, or recurrent symptoms needs medical care.

biologyPathway.title: Vaginal Ecosystem
biologyPathway.detail: The vaginal ecosystem relies on pH, lactobacilli, hormones, immune function, and tissue integrity. Yes, it is basically a tiny biochemical city council.

biologyPathway.title: Common Causes
biologyPathway.short: BV, yeast, trichomoniasis, irritants, and low estrogen can all mimic each other.
biologyPathway.detail: BV often causes thin discharge and odor; yeast often causes itching and thick white discharge; trichomoniasis can cause frothy yellow-green discharge and requires partner treatment.

biologyPathway.title: Wrong Treatment Problem
biologyPathway.detail: Antifungal cream will not treat BV or trichomoniasis, and antibiotics can worsen yeast. Civilization invented testing for a reason.

biologyPathway.title: Recurrence Screen
biologyPathway.detail: Recurrent vaginitis may involve diabetes, immune suppression, hormones, sexual exposure, products, antibiotics, douching, or resistant/recurrent infection.

sections.heading: Stage 1 — Vaginitis Is a Category, Not One Disease
body: Vaginitis means inflammation or irritation of vaginal/vulvar tissue.
The cause can be BV, yeast, trichomoniasis, STI-related, allergic/irritant, low estrogen, pregnancy/hormonal, diabetes-related, or another condition.

sections.heading: Stage 2 — Symptoms Overlap
body: Itching, burning, discharge, odor, sex pain, and urinary discomfort can overlap between causes.
This is why guessing is a spectacularly annoying medical strategy.

sections.heading: Stage 3 — Testing and Treatment
body: Treatment depends on the cause: antibiotics, antifungals, STI treatment, estrogen therapy, irritant removal, diabetes management, or other care.
Trichomoniasis requires partner treatment. BV and yeast do not follow the same rules.

sections.heading: Stage 4 — Safety Check
body: Seek care for fever, pelvic pain, pregnancy, foul odor, green/yellow discharge, sores, bleeding, severe pain, STI concern, or recurrent symptoms.
Do not turn pelvic infection signs into a breathing exercise.

witty.label: Comedic & Witty
witty.title: The Walled Garden With Bad Landscaping
paragraphs: Your vaginal ecosystem is a walled garden with flowers, weeds, pH rules, and a gardener who is one bad antibiotic away from quitting.
The lactobacilli are the flowers. BV bacteria are the weeds. Candida is the thistle. Trichomoniasis is not a weed; it is an STI goblin with paperwork.
Stress, douching, scented products, antibiotics, hormones, sex, diabetes, and immune changes can all mess with the garden.
The answer is not throwing random cream over the fence.
The answer is figuring out what is actually growing there, then treating it correctly.
The garden deserves testing, not vibes in a tube.

brutal.label: Brutally Honest
brutal.title: The Listen Before It Burns Reality Check
paragraphs: You may have a vaginitis problem. You may also have a neglected-signal pattern.
Medical first: vaginitis needs cause-based diagnosis. BV, yeast, trich, irritation, low estrogen, and STIs do not all use the same treatment.
Symbolically, ask what signal you kept dismissing until the body got louder.
What changed: product, partner, antibiotic, blood sugar, hormone shift, stress, soap, douching, or sex pattern?
Pay attention early. Get tested when needed. Stop guessing with drugstore roulette.
Restore the garden properly or keep letting irritation run the neighborhood council. Your choice.

influenceLayers.title: Liver-Spleen Damp-Heat
paragraphs: In Chinese medicine language, damp-heat can symbolize irritation, discharge, heat, and imbalance in the lower body.
Medically, the cause still needs diagnosis. The microscope outranks the metaphor.

influenceLayers.title: Self-Caring One
paragraphs: The Self-Caring One intervenes early.
She does not wait until the garden is screaming.

influenceLayers.title: Boundary and Products
paragraphs: This layer asks what is being allowed into a sensitive ecosystem.
Some things simply do not belong there, and apparently scented soap needed to be told.

influenceLayers.title: Microbiome Balance
paragraphs: Balance is not sterility.
The goal is a healthy ecosystem, not nuking everything from orbit.

influenceLayers.title: Attention Before Escalation
paragraphs: The reset is noticing, testing, treating correctly, and removing triggers.
The garden improves when the gardener stops winging it.

reset.title: Pelvic Ecosystem Grounding
steps: Sit upright with one hand on the lower belly and one on the heart.
Inhale through the nose for 4 seconds.
Exhale through the mouth for 8 seconds.
Whisper: I listen early. I restore balance with care, not shame.
Then choose one practical action: stop irritants, avoid douching, track symptoms, schedule testing, or follow prescribed treatment.

naturalSupport.disclaimer: Supportive care is not a cure. Vaginitis treatment depends on the cause and may require testing, prescriptions, STI treatment, or pregnancy-safe care.

Listen before the downstairs garden starts filing formal complaints.

clinical.mechanism: Vaginitis is inflammation or irritation of the vagina/vulva. Causes can include bacterial vaginosis, yeast infection, trichomoniasis, STIs, irritants/allergies, low estrogen, antibiotics, diabetes, pregnancy, hormonal changes, immune suppression, or other gynecologic conditions.
protocol: Track discharge, odor, itching, burning, pelvic pain, urinary symptoms, bleeding, products, sex, STI risk, pregnancy, diabetes symptoms, antibiotics, and recurrence.
Seek diagnosis before repeating treatment.
Seek care promptly for pelvic pain, fever, pregnancy, severe pain, bleeding, sores, STI concern, or recurrent symptoms.
